title: Implement backpressure signal frames and per-stream pressure state
purpose: Implements protocol-level backpressure as defined by ADR-004. Accept, Defer, and Reject are distinct frame types that travel on the same stream as the messages they regulate (P5). Per-stream pressure state tracks outstanding (unacknowledged) messages and transitions between normal, deferred, and rejecting states. Consumers declare their capacity (max-in-flight) during subscription, giving the bus the information it needs to mediate between producer rate and consumer capacity.
task: Build the backpressure module. Define the Accept, Defer, and Reject frame payload fields (referenced message ID, stream ID enforcement on the same stream). Implement per-stream pressure state tracking: a state machine with states Normal, Deferred, Rejecting that transitions based on outstanding message count relative to the consumer's declared max_in_flight. Wire max_in_flight into the Subscribe frame so the bus knows consumer capacity. Scope boundary: this brief handles backpressure signal types and per-stream pressure tracking. It does not implement the bus-level policies that decide when to shed load or spawn consumers — those are bus-layer concerns above the protocol.

requirements.title: Define Accept, Defer, and Reject frame payloads
requirements.spec: Accept, Defer, and Reject frame payloads SHALL carry a referenced_message_id (a unique identifier for the message being acknowledged/deferred/rejected). These frames SHALL travel on the same stream as the original message (CN8) — the frame's stream_id in the header identifies which subscription the pressure signal applies to. Each frame type SHALL have a distinct FrameType discriminant (defined in PROTO-001). The payload SHALL also carry an optional reason string for Defer and Reject.
acceptance: Defer payload has fields: referenced_message_id, reason (Option<String>).
Reject payload has fields: referenced_message_id, reason (Option<String>).
All three frame types use their PROTO-001 FrameType discriminant.
All three frame types round-trip through the codec.

requirements.title: Implement per-stream pressure state machine
requirements.spec: PressureState enum SHALL have variants: Normal, Deferred, Rejecting. StreamPressure SHALL track: outstanding_count (messages delivered but not acknowledged), max_in_flight (from subscription), and current PressureState. WHEN outstanding_count reaches max_in_flight, THE SYSTEM SHALL transition to Deferred. WHEN outstanding_count exceeds max_in_flight plus the stream's buffer capacity, THE SYSTEM SHALL transition to Rejecting. WHEN outstanding_count drops below max_in_flight (via Accept), THE SYSTEM SHALL transition back to Normal.
acceptance: PressureState has exactly 3 variants: Normal, Deferred, Rejecting.
StreamPressure with max_in_flight=10 transitions to Deferred when outstanding reaches 10.
StreamPressure transitions back to Normal when outstanding drops below max_in_flight after Accept.
StreamPressure tracks outstanding_count incrementing on delivery, decrementing on Accept.

requirements.title: Wire max_in_flight into Subscribe frame
requirements.spec: The Subscribe frame payload SHALL include a max_in_flight (u32) field declaring the consumer's processing capacity. This value SHALL be stored in the StreamPressure when the subscription becomes active. A max_in_flight of 0 SHALL be rejected with ProtocolError — every consumer must declare a capacity. The bus uses this value to determine when to send Defer/Reject signals.
acceptance: Subscribe with max_in_flight=100 creates a StreamPressure with that capacity.
Subscribe frame round-trips through the codec with max_in_flight preserved.
max_in_flight is accessible from the decoded Subscribe frame.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement bus-level pressure policies (spawn consumers, shed load) — those are above the protocol.
SHALL NOT implement rate limiting or throttling — the protocol signals pressure, it does not enforce rates.
SHALL NOT define buffer capacity thresholds — those are configurable by the bus, not hardcoded in the protocol.
SHALL NOT modify backpressure signals to travel on a different stream than the messages they regulate (P5).
verification: cargo check compiles the backpressure module with no errors.
Test: Accept/Defer/Reject frames round-trip through codec.
Test: StreamPressure transitions Normal -> Deferred at max_in_flight.
Test: StreamPressure transitions Deferred -> Normal on Accept reducing outstanding below max_in_flight.
Test: Subscribe with max_in_flight=0 is rejected.
